name: ExcelJS (Node.js Excel Spreadsheet Library)

performance.notes: Large files can be memory-intensive; streaming write available

agent_readiness.notes: Node.js Excel (.xlsx) spreadsheet read/write. const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook(). workbook.addWorksheet('Sheet1'). worksheet.addRow([1, 'Alice', 100]). Styling: cell.fill, cell.font, cell.border. Conditional formatting. Charts (limited). Images. Streaming write for large files. Password protection. Formulas. worksheet.columns for column definitions. Read: workbook.xlsx.readFile(). Better than xlsx package for styling. TypeScript definitions included.

gotchas: Chart support is limited vs Excel native charts
Large files require streaming API to avoid OOM
Some complex Excel features (pivot tables) not supported
File size grows quickly with images/styling
best_when: Generating styled Excel reports from Node.js
Business report generation with formatting, borders, colors
Exporting data tables to Excel with column headers
avoid_when: Simple tabular export (CSV + xlsx package simpler)
summary: ExcelJS generates and reads .xlsx files in Node.js with full styling support. workbook.addWorksheet(), worksheet.addRow(), cell styling (fill, font, border, alignment). Streaming write for large files. TypeScript types included. Better than xlsx package for formatting. Read existing files too. The go-to for Node.js Excel report generation with rich formatting.
